The rule is: each non-empty row has one symmetric red pair, and the output fills the row segment for the pair with the smallest horizontal gap. I discarded both summary hints because example 2 breaks their outer-row/global-extreme reading; that correction is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_5ad8a7c0/notes.md#L1). The generator matches the task family by scaling the same sparse symmetric-endpoint pattern to variable even widths and heights, with row-level span walks and optional empty rows; no new primitive was needed because `connect` and `fill` were sufficient.
